# Aesop's Fables

Short fables are ideal first lessons in narration, moral reasoning, and concise writing.

## Spelling Words

| Core Words | Challenge Words |
|---|---|
| fable | consequence |
| moral | patience |
| fox | gratitude |
| lion | deception |
| mouse | generosity |
| crow | foolishness |
| grapes | quarrel |
| boast | kindness |
| trick | justice |
| wise | humility |

## Word Study

- Sort words by short vowel and long vowel sounds.
- Find words that name virtues and vices.
- Write three original sentences using one animal, one action, and one moral word.

## Narration

- Retell one fable in three sentences: beginning, turning point, and lesson.
- Name the character who changes least and explain why.
- State the moral without using the exact traditional wording.

## Writing Prompts

- Write a new fable about two animals who want the same thing.
- Explain the difference between cleverness and wisdom.
- Choose one fable and write its lesson as advice to a younger child.

## Projects

- Make a small illustrated fable booklet with five fables and five morals.
- Perform a fable with simple narration and two speaking parts.
- Create a virtue-and-vice chart from ten fables.
